title: ipomopsis congesta subsp. viridis
intro: Ipomopsis congesta subsp. viridis, also known as the green or green skyrocket, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family. Native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing skin irritations due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds. The leaves and flowers of Ipomopsis congesta subsp. viridis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, although caution is advised when using the plant due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
